title: Collinear ferromagnetism with reduced moment length in kagome magnet Nd3Ru4Al12
authors: Yuki Ishihara, Ryota Nakano, Rinsuke Yamada, Takuya Nomoto, Priya R. Baral, Moritz M. Hirschmann, Kamini Gautam, Kamil K. Kolincio, Akiko Kikkawa, Seno Aji, Hiraku Saitoh, Masaaki Matsuda, Yasujiro Taguchi, Taka-hisa Arima, Yoshinori Tokura, Taro Nakajima, Max Hirschberger
abstract: We determine the magnetic ground state of the kagome lattice magnet Nd3Ru4Al12 by single-crystal neutron diffraction, supported by experiments with polarized neutrons. We identify this material as a collinear ferromagnet ("hex-FM") with uniform moment length mc = 2.1 μB/Nd and ordering vector Q = 0, in contrast to a previous, seminal report that proposed unequal moment lengths on two Nd sites, here called the "ortho-FM" state. Our analysis of the flipping ratio in polarized neutron scattering is consistent with the hex-FM state. The results provide a microscopic basis for understanding the large fluctuation-induced Hall and Nernst responses near TC = 41 K, as previously reported for Nd3Ru4Al12.
summary: This paper uses neutron diffraction to prove that the kagome magnet Nd3Ru4Al12 has a collinear hexagonal ferromagnetic ground state rather than a previously proposed orthorhombic one. This finding provides a microscopic basis for the large Hall and Nernst responses observed near the transition temperature. The result resolves a long-standing discrepancy in the magnetic modeling of this material.
main_problem: Resolving the discrepancy regarding the magnetic ground state of the kagome lattice magnet Nd3Ru4Al12, specifically whether it follows a previously reported orthorhombic 'ortho-FM' state or a simpler hexagonal state.
main_result: The study identifies the ground state as a collinear hexagonal ferromagnetic (hex-FM) state with uniform moment length, effectively refuting the existence of the previously proposed orthorhombic state.
method: Single-crystal neutron diffraction (unpolarized and polarized) was used to analyze magnetic structure factors and flipping ratios, supplemented by DFT calculations.
model_or_system: The physical system is the kagome lattice magnet Nd3Ru4Al12, characterized by a staggered Nd sublattice and a hexagonal ferromagnetic (hex-FM) magnetic space group.
key_observables: Magnetic structure factors, magnetic intensity at (100) and (200) reflections, flipping ratio (P_obs), and ordered magnetic moment (mc).
important_parameters: Critical temperature Tc approx 41 K, magnetic moment mc approx 2.1-2.3 uB/Nd, and ordering vector Q = 0.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the nuclear structure refinement to isolate the magnetic signal.
figures_summary: Fig 1: Kagome lattice and fluctuation mechanism; Fig 2: Unpolarized neutron scattering intensities; Fig 3: Comparison of observed vs calculated structure factors; Fig 4: Comparison of different magnetic models (hex-FM, AOAO, ortho-FM); Fig 5: Polarized neutron scattering geometry and flipping ratio results.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific conflict, describes the experimental neutron diffraction setup, presents the structural refinement and symmetry analysis, compares competing magnetic models, and concludes with the implications for understanding thermal spin fluctuations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (color online). Ferromagnetic order in R3Ru4Al12(R = Pr, Nd) with kagome lattice structure for the rare earth sublattice (’hex-FM’). (a) Ferromagnetic ordering as reported here for R = Nd (hex-FM). The staggered kagome sublattice of Nd is depicted by the projection. Magenta (Nd) and grey (Ru) atoms, but not Al atoms, are shown. (b) Ferromagnetic (TC) and antiferromagnetic (TN) transition temperatures of R3Ru4Al12 as a function of the de-Gennes factor. For conduction-electron mediated magnetic interactions, we expect a linear relation. R = Pr, Nd are clear outliers with a sizable net magnetization. (c) Fluctuation-induced Hall effect in Nd3Ru4Al12 at and above TC, as reported in Ref....

FIG. 2. (color online). Unpolarized neutron scattering on Nd3Ru4Al12. The nuclear scattering intensity Inuc from the crystallographic structure (grey, T = 120 K), magnetic scattering intensity Imag (blue), and error of the magnetic intensity ∆Imag (magenta bars) are indicated. The Miller indices (HKL) are indicated above each dataset. We obtain Imag from T = 2 K diffraction data I2 K by subtracting Inuc. The square root is taken for the absolute value of Iobs and multiplied by the sign of Iobs. The error bars are calculated as a statistical uncertainty of the fit to ω −2θ scans, but required to be larger than 5 % of I2 K. (a), Inset: Geometry of beamline PONTA-5G of JRR-3 for this...

FIG. 3. (color online). Refinement of magnetic and crystallographic structure of Nd3Ru4Al12 by comparison of Fobs and Fcalc, the observed and calculated structure factors, respectively. (a) Magnetic structure factor in the (HK0) plane. Inset, hex-FM: collinear ferromagnetic structure of magnetic neodymium ions (magenta) in the hexagonal ferromagnetic state. (b) Nuclear scattering related to the crystal structure of Nd3Ru4Al12. The ratio of the slopes (red lines) of the two plots yields the ordered moment of the Nd ion, mc = 2.3 µB. On the right lower side, we give the refined intracell coordinates (in lattice units) for Nd on the 6h Wyckoff site and for Al on the 4f, 6h, and 12k Wyckoff...

FIG. 4. (color online). Magnetic structure models compared along the (H00) line in reciprocal space. (a) Hexagonal ferromag- netic state (hex-FM) with uniform moment length on all sites. Only Nd ions (magenta) are shown. (b) All-in-all-out canted ferromagnetic state, as discussed in the text. (c) Orthorhombic ferromagnetic state (ortho-FM) with two different sites for Nd, as proposed in Ref. [17]. (d) Comparison of observed (blue bars with magenta errors) and calculated (red / green lines) magnetic scattering intensity along (H00). FIG. 5. (color online). Polarized neutron scattering on Nd3Ru4Al12 in the (HK0) scattering plane. (a) Experimental geometry. As compared to Fig. 2, a spin flipper, Helmholtz coil, and Heusler analyzer for the neutron spin polarization were added (red). The spin of the incoming neutron can be “up” or “down”, whereas the analyzer selects “up” only. (b,c) Rocking scans through two reflections with relatively strong scattering intensity Idu when the spin flipper is “ON”. (d) Flipping ratio Pobs = (Idu −Iuu)/(Idu + Iuu) compared to our calculation result for the hex-FM state, when taking account for neutron beam depolarization p.
